claims.text: Manufacturer contact, design upload, and quote requests remain behind explicit authorization.
A guided two-piece sliding carrier is the strongest concept-level direction.
Putting the full travel into four M6 slots leaves about 1.7 mm at the outer ends and is too fragile for this alignment-critical mount.
The listed base, carrier, slots, guide depth, and stack thickness form a plausible starting package.
Final support geometry cannot be selected until shaft orientation relative to the machine plate is confirmed.
A design-factor-three screen produces 450 N radial load, 11.25 N·m bending, and 9 N·m reaction torque.
The provisional four-clamp friction screen gives about 3,000 N translational and 116 N·m moment capacity.
The four M5 motor fasteners appear unlikely to govern under simple load distribution.
The preliminary calculations do not validate deflection, separation, plate flexibility, vibration retention, or motor threads.
Three prototypes should start as 3-axis CNC-machined 6061-T6 plates at the listed thicknesses.
CNC machining offers more predictable guide geometry and is likely more economical than a precision welded assembly for this concept.
Black anodizing should remain optional and sliding/pilot datums need masking or compensation if selected.
The internal review package should contain requirements, parametric source, STEP AP242, drawings, BOM, procedures, inspection, and validation plans.
The listed dimensions, clearances, parallelism, flatness, pilot clearance, and datum controls are provisional drawing controls.
Dimensional inspection, travel, tolerance, stiffness, cycling, and no-slip proof testing remain required and unperformed.
The listed local-shop, PCBWay, Xometry, and Protolabs routes are provisional capability-fit candidates.
No candidate's current capability, price, availability, or delivery was checked.
A provider must pass the listed budget, delivery, material, process, inspection, confidentiality, and shipping gates.
The provisional acceptance target is at most 0.10 mm transverse shift and 0.1° angular change after locking and proof loading.
Internal CAD and drawing development is proposed only behind the quoted approval.
Read-only supplier research is proposed behind a separate approval and excludes disclosure or quoting.

constraintAssessments.notes: No CAD artifact, interference check, structural validation, dimensional inspection, or physical bracket exists; concept geometry is explicitly unvalidated.

Loads and a nominal envelope were supplied, but shaft orientation, travel direction, full envelope interpretation, detailed motor dimensions, plate support, and numerical alignment acceptance remain unresolved.
Only a package approach was described; no editable model, STEP export, drawing, DFM receipt, tolerance contract, or inspection artifact was created.
Candidate routes remain provisional. Exact process/material/feature fit, dimensions, quantity, price, Singapore delivery, lead time, and inspection are not live-verified.
The captain established three prototypes, USD 250 before shipping, Singapore delivery, and a three-week target; provider ability to meet them remains unknown.
The captain explicitly prohibited disclosure, quote, payment, order, and fabrication; all handoffs are proposed and separately approval-gated.
